hero_subtitle: SFTY Government centralizes and structures digital asset data streams to support regulators in managing audit-ready reporting, case workflows, and aligned supervisory documentation.
trust_card_1_value: Market participants, licensing data and risk profiles
trust_card_2_value: Wallets, transactions and exposure across the market
trust_card_3_value: Originator and beneficiary data checked for completeness
trust_card_5_value: On-premise or private cloud for sensitive data
trust_card_6_value: Supervisory outputs, evidence and regulatory reports
problem_title: Regulatory reporting remains disconnected and manual
problem_description: Regulators struggle with siloed data sources and fragmented workflows that slow down regulatory reporting and reduce audit readiness. This makes it difficult to produce consistent, evidence-backed reports that align with evolving supervisory frameworks and jurisdictional requirements.
problem_point_1_value: VASP activity, licensing data and risk signals remain scattered
problem_point_2_value: Alerts and investigations depend on disconnected manual processes
problem_point_3_value: Decisions are harder to explain without connected logs and case history
problem_point_4_value: Regulatory teams lack one shared operating layer for supervision
solution_description: SFTY connects fragmented data, supervisory cases, evidentiary records and multi-source inputs into a unified regulatory reporting workflow tailored for government teams pursuing audit-ready outputs.
workflow_title: From market signal to supervisory report
workflow_description: SFTY connects market signals, alerts, cases and evidence to structured supervisory actions and audit-ready regulatory reports for government teams.
workflow_step_1_description: Wallet, VASP, Travel Rule or OSINT signal appears
workflow_step_2_description: SFTY classifies and routes the signal for review
workflow_step_3_description: A supervisory case opens with context & evidence
workflow_step_4_description: Teams record a decision, request or supervisory response
workflow_step_5_description: Evidence, actions and outcomes stay available for review
why_sfty_title: Not another analytics tool. Supervisory records connected
why_sfty_description: SFTY helps regulators move beyond fragmented data by linking market activity, licensing, cases and evidence into structured workflows for reliable regulatory reporting.
why_sfty_card_1_description: See activity across VASPs, wallets, entities and risk signals
why_sfty_card_2_description: Connect participants, structures, links and supervisory records
why_sfty_card_3_description: Move from alerts to investigations, actions and reporting
why_sfty_card_4_description: Understand not only single risks, but the condition of the market
authority_description: SFTY supports regulators with secure deployment, controlled access and evidence-grade logging to support structured, auditable regulatory reporting within sovereign environments.
authority_card_1_description: Deploy on-premise, in private cloud or inside a controlled government environment
authority_card_2_description: Preserve activity, decisions, case history and reporting evidence
authority_card_3_description: Control who can view, review, escalate and act across teams and agencies
authority_card_4_description: Support phased implementation, documentation, integrations and governance review
ask_sfty_placeholder: Ask SFTY how to structure audit-ready regulatory reports
ask_sfty_chip_1_message: Explain how regulatory data flows into supervisory case records
ask_sfty_chip_2_message: Show how evidence-grade logging supports regulatory report reviews
ask_sfty_chip_3_message: Describe how SFTY aligns reporting with jurisdictional standards
ask_sfty_helper_text: AI assistant for supervisory, deployment and compliance questions
faq_description: Clear answers on VASP supervision, sovereign deployment, evidence logging and regulatory workflows.
faq_1_question: What is SFTY for reporting workflows?
faq_1_answer: SFTY provides a structured, auditable supervisory layer that connects digital asset data, cases, evidence and reports to support reporting workflows and align with jurisdictional frameworks.
faq_2_question: How does SFTY handle supervisory case workflows?
faq_2_answer: SFTY structures alerts into risk-classified cases with linked evidence, decisions and reporting workflows, enabling regulators to manage supervisory lifecycle from detection to reporting consistently.
faq_3_question: How does SFTY preserve audit-ready evidence?
faq_3_answer: SFTY preserves case history, decision logs, supporting documents and supervisory actions within secure, audit logs to support review readiness and regulatory oversight.
faq_4_question: Can SFTY integrate with existing government systems?
faq_4_answer: SFTY supports modular integration with government registries, reporting systems and data providers, enabling controlled access, audit logging and phased deployment within secure government environments.
faq_5_question: Does SFTY replace existing regulatory tools?
faq_5_answer: No. SFTY operates above existing regulatory analytics and compliance providers, connecting their outputs into connected workflows without replacing underlying tools or data sources.
final_description: Review how SFTY supports connected case management, supervisory records, and evidence-based reporting workflows for public authorities.
footer_description: Supervisory and compliance control layer for digital asset markets, businesses and regulated institutions.
footer_contact_title: For supervisory, compliance and deployment discussions.
footer_legal_notice: This page is for informational purposes only and does not constitute legal, regulatory or compliance advice. SFTY deployment depends on jurisdiction, client requirements and applicable laws.
